initiatives.name: The Good Life Center
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community power through sustainable living education and cultural preservation in Maine. They coordinate homesteading workshops, skill sharing, and solidarity networks while building power through ecological wisdom, simple living, and creating spaces for learning about self-reliant and sustainable lifestyles.

initiatives.name: Church of Safe Injection
initiatives.description: A grassroots harm reduction organization fostering mutual aid and community care through direct support in Maine. They coordinate harm reduction supplies distribution, overdose prevention education, and solidarity networks while building power through peer support, direct action, and creating spaces for compassionate care and dignity.

initiatives.name: SURJ of the Midcoast
initiatives.description: A chapter of Showing Up for Racial Justice fostering mutual aid and community solidarity through white anti-racist organizing in Midcoast Maine. They coordinate political education, direct action support, and solidarity networks while building power through racial justice education and accountable organizing in partnership with BIPOC-led organizations.

initiatives.name: MaineTransNet
initiatives.description: MaineTransNet is a community based organization led by transgender people for transgender people. We provide peer-to-peer support groups, social and community events, advocacy for the transgender community across Maine, and training for medical, mental health, and social service providers.

initiatives.name: Out in The Open
initiatives.description: Out in the Open connects rural LGBTQ+ people to build community, visibility, knowledge and power. We are a multiracial, majority working class, grassroots, movement and capacity building organization based in Wabanaki territory throughout the states of Vermont and Maine.

initiatives.name: Maine DSA
initiatives.description: Our chapter primarily organizes in the Portland Metropolitan area, although our campaigns and projects may operate statewide and our membership draws from every county in the state.
